steps.title: Identify the relationship shift
steps.content:
The passage's crucial moment comes when Adam's “affectionate willingness to fall in with his own plans” wins the old man's heart.

His flexibility—staying on the ship, being ready at any hour, agreeing to go anywhere—directly transforms them from formal relatives into “like old friends.”

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
It showcases how Adam's flexibility and consideration strengthen his relationship with his great-uncle Richard. directly states the passage's purpose: showcasing how Adam's flexibility and consideration strengthen the relationship.

The uncle's transformation proves that his willingness to adapt is what opens the old man's heart.

The other three choices either focus on journey excitement, invent initial wariness, or reduce to a practical accommodation detail.

That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
